title: Today's Tech Brief: How to watch Nvidia’s Computex keynote
summary: A look at Nvidia’s Computex keynote and the Pope’s thoughts on AI
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for How to watch Nvidia’s Computex keynote
imageCaption: How to watch Nvidia’s Computex keynote (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I've got a couple of interesting tech stories to share with you today. First up, Nvidia is hosting its Computex keynote tonight at 8PM PT / 11PM ET. I'm curious to see if Jensen Huang will announce the rumored N1 and N1X ARM CPUs. I've been following Nvidia for a while now, and it's clear that they're a major player in the industry.

According to The Verge, Nvidia's Computex keynote is happening tonight. My read is that this could be a big deal for the tech world, especially if Huang announces those new ARM CPUs. This could potentially shake up the market, and I'm excited to see how it all plays out.

Also making headlines is the Pope's new encyclical on artificial intelligence, Magnifica Humanitas. As reported by MIT Technology Review, the Pope is emphasizing that technology is never neutral. What stands out to me here is that this could be a turning point in how we think about AI and its impact on society. It's interesting to see a religious leader weighing in on the topic, and I'm curious to see how this influences the conversation.

Another news item that caught my eye is the ongoing discussion around AI and its effects on our daily lives. From smart home devices to AI-powered cars, it's clear that this tech is becoming increasingly prevalent. I'm looking forward to seeing how this develops and what new innovations come out of it.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: How to watch Nvidia’s Computex keynote
How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
